summary.brief: Rodriguez v. Diede. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: In January 2018, Rodriguez was driving a 2017 Chevrolet Silverado truck at about thirty miles per hour eastbound on 6200 South in West Jordan, Utah.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rodriguez reported that the day after the accident, she began to experience headaches and pain in her neck, shoulder, and arm.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rodriguez’s auto insurance carrier paid $3,000 from personal injury protection (PIP) coverage toward Rodriguez’s bills from Chiropractor.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Chiropractor and Doctor each also opined that Rodriguez’s and Ramirez’s injuries and symptoms were caused by the accident.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Diede called a biomechanical engineer, who testified that the accident caused “a speed transfer of about 1.6 miles per hour on [Diede’s] vehicle and about 2.1 miles per hour on [Rodriguez’s] vehicle.”
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Diede then called a radiologist who reviewed Rodriguez’s and Ramirez’s MRI scans and x-rays and said he saw no “objective evidence” to suggest that the accident caused injury to Rodriguez’s spine or disc herniations to Ramirez.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Diede called an “academic orthopedic spine surgeon” who opined that Doctor’s recommendation for Rodriguez to have neck surgery was “way below the standard of care” because there were no medical indications to support a need for surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The jury found that “Diede’s fault [was not] a cause of the harm claimed by [Rodriguez and Ramirez],” and it therefore did not reach the question of damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Jury finding

evidence_index.text: We conclude that the district court’s admission of testimony regarding Rodriguez’s lack of awareness regarding the cost of her medical treatments and Doctor’s testimony about receiving payment from Intermountain did not violate the collateral source rule.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.chief_complaint: Headaches and pain in neck, shoulder, and arm
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Rodriguez reported that the day after the accident, she began to experience headaches and pain in her neck, shoulder, and arm. She stated that she had trouble sleeping and that her shoulder, neck, and head pain made it difficult for her to do ordinary household tasks.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

reveal.result_summary: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

requestables.name: Chiropractor Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with a chiropractor for neck and shoulder pain

reveal.result_summary: Chiropractor referred Rodriguez for MRI and to a pain specialist for injections

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes MRI and specialist consultations

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with persistent neck and shoulder pain post-accident
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.clinical_reasoning: Surgery was recommended by Doctor but disputed by defense experts

options.description: Continue conservative management with physical therapy
options.clinical_reasoning: Defense experts opined surgery was not indicated

expected_action_court.description: Conservative management should have been continued
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated surgery was not necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Surgery was not supported by objective findings
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Conservative management should have been pursued

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent neck surgery and reported no lasting pain post-surgery

key_findings: Jury found Diede's fault was not a cause of the harm claimed

court_findings.finding: No causation between accident and claimed injuries
